title: Signal Dry Bulk Weekly Report
summary:
Main
In the fourth week of November, the weaker dynamics of freight market rates have yet to be reversed in all ship size classes, while demand continues to signal a downward trend. Economic uncertainties for the Chinese economy and the escalation of geopolitical tensions between Russia and Ukraine appear to be shaking the strength of freight rates, as the number of ships sailing in ballast for supramax and handysize vessels has declined significantly from the increase recorded in early November. As shown in the chart below based on Signal Ocean Data, the vessel speed for seagoing ballasted and loaded vessels has dropped to its lowest level compared to a similar period in the previous two years as freight rates continue to decline and demand has not yet turned to positive momentum.

In the fourth week of November, the weaker dynamics of freight market rates have yet to be reversed in all ship size classes, while demand continues to signal a downward trend. Economic uncertainties for the Chinese economy and the escalation of geopolitical tensions between Russia and Ukraine appear to be shaking the strength of freight rates, as the number of ships sailing in ballast for supramax and handysize vessels has declined significantly from the increase recorded in early November.

children.section_path_text: Chart of the Week Dry Bulk: Vessel Speed
children.summary:
Current levels are at the lowest compared to a similar period in 2021-2020
Image reference: unnamed - 2022-11-23T211959.927.png | assets/2022-11-24_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2022-11-23t211959927_de1e106d1bba.png
Data Source: The Signal Ocean Platform, Vessel Speed
https://go.signalocean.com/e/983831/dry-reports-vesselSpeeds/2l66vj/287121756?h=3E2GAJ3d3YyNT46BsvLMBg5xZZrLppARiyEPCZQGeFU

children.title: SECTION 1/ FREIGHT - Market Rates ($/t) Weaker
children.summary:
‘The Big Picture' - Capesize and Panamax Bulkers and Smaller Ship Sizes
Image reference: unnamed - 2022-11-23T212105.517.png | assets/2022-11-24_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2022-11-23t212105517_d4a48f262bbc.png
In the last days of November, the weaker sentiment from the beginning of the month continues, while a sustained downward trend seems to emerge in the following days. Capesize vessel freight rates are approaching $18/tonne, down nearly $2/tonne from two weeks ago and down $7/tonne from week 40.

children.title: SECTION 2/ SUPPLY - Ballasters (# vessels) Increasing
children.summary:
Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas
Image reference: unnamed - 2022-11-23T212244.084.png | assets/2022-11-24_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2022-11-23t212244084_c85efc46a098.png
The number of ships in ballast was down only in the Panamax segment, while the other ship size classes showed an accelerated increase compared to the previous weeks of November. Capesize SE Africa: The number of vessels now stands at 87, 10 more than in Week 45 and 7 above the annual average.

children.title: SECTION 3/ DEMAND - TonDays Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed - 2022-11-24T123725.014.png | assets/2022-11-24_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2022-11-24t123725014_9933e3c762ec.png
Demand growth continues to decline, with stronger signs in the Panamax segment, while the trend in the Capesize segment is clearly downward. Capesize demand ton-days: Compared to the highs of week 41, the trend has not yet recovered in the fourth week of November.

children.title: SECTION 4/ PORT CONGESTION - No of Vessels Increasing
section_path: SECTION 4/ PORT CONGESTION - No of Vessels Increasing
children.section_path_text: SECTION 4/ PORT CONGESTION - No of Vessels Increasing
children.summary:
Dry bulk ships congested at Chinese ports
Image reference: unnamed - 2022-11-24T123920.556.png | assets/2022-11-24_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed-2022-11-24t123920556_ac50db9e9469.png
This week, the new highs in total bulk carrier congestion are led by the Panamax, Supramax, and Handysize segments. Capesize: The number of vessels decreased to 128, 5 less than the previous week.
